description: '''Frat rap''' is a [[subgenre]] of [[hip hop music]] that emerged in the late 2000s. It is sonically easygoing with a straightforward rhyming style and singsong cadence accompanied by a lyrical focal point on [[coming of age]] hedonism, which can include [[campus]] lifestyle, debaucherous [[parties]], [[recreational drugs]], and women. Frat rap quickly became commercially popular among a predominately white, college-aged, suburban male audience in the northeastern United States. [[Asher Roth]]'s 2009 song "[[I Love College]]" is considered to have initiated the subgenre. Notable frat rappers such as [[Mac Miller]] and [[Lil Dicky]] later became successful in mainstream hip hop.
